mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents through standardized communication protocols. It simplifies deployment complexity, enhances security, and reduces operational overhead, making it ideal for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and familiarity with Helm for installation.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Kagenti CLI tool to manage deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

```

> This command downloads the CLI, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to connect to the new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the new cluster's credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their status.

steps.title: Install Kagenti
steps.description:
Deploy Kagenti using Helm to manage the installation.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Install Kagenti in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/examples/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies an example autoscaler configuration.
> This command applies an example monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
Remove the Kagenti deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data managed by Kagenti.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

gcloud container clusters delete kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the installation, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all --namespace kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti

```

> Backup current resources to a YAML file.
> Upgrade to the latest version of Kagenti.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all --namespace kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (NotFound): namespaces "kagenti" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kagenti namespace was not created or was deleted.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl create namespace kagenti' to create the namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image "kagenti/kagenti:0.5.1": rpc error: code = NotFound desc = manifest for kagenti/kagenti:0.5.1 not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image version does not exist in the container registry.

**Fix:**
Check the Kagenti GitHub releases for the correct image version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on Kagenti pods.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing required environment variables.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace kagenti' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient cpu' or 'Insufficient memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new agent communication protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods --namespace kagenti'. All pods should be in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/kagenti

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
